success_surface: Selective cell transformation with type-aware filtering preserved data integrity
effective_behaviors: Pre-execution data exploration to understand actual cell value types before applying transformations
Use openpyxl for cell-by-cell modifications when selective updates are needed within a range
Pattern-based filtering using regex to target specific value formats while excluding other data types
Explicit null handling to skip empty cells safely during iteration
Scope control using task-defined answer_position to limit processing range precisely
Post-execution verification reporting change counts for sanity check
evidence: Agent ran exploratory code first to identify date values, alphanumeric strings, and numeric values in column B before transformation
Used load_workbook() from openpyxl instead of pandas.read_excel() to preserve workbook structure
Applied regex pattern r'^[A-Z]+ ?(\d+)$' to selectively match only alphanumeric codes needing modification
Checked 'if cell_value is None' before attempting string conversion or regex matching
Limited processing loop to start_row=14 and end_row=137 based on answer_position A14:I137
Printed processed_count after completion to validate transformation results

memory_items.title: Explore Before Transform
memory_items.lesson: Always examine actual cell value types and distributions before applying bulk transformations to prevent unintended side effects on non-target data.
memory_items.generalization: For sheet-level manipulations, run an inspection pass to categorize cell contents (dates, strings, numbers, None) before implementing the modification logic.

memory_items.title: Pattern-Based Cell Selection
memory_items.lesson: Use regular expressions or conditional checks to identify target cells by content pattern rather than position alone, ensuring non-matching data remains untouched.
memory_items.generalization: When modifying cells containing mixed data types, apply content-based filters that naturally exclude protected values like dates or static numbers.

memory_items.title: OpenPyXL for Selective Updates
memory_items.lesson: Use openpyxl's iterative cell access when making targeted edits to preserve formulas and formatting that pandas would otherwise lose.
memory_items.generalization: For operations requiring cell-level precision within a defined range, iterate directly on the worksheet object rather than converting to DataFrame and back.

edits.content:
## Trace-Derived Lessons

### Pre-Execution Inspection
- **Inspect Before Modify:** Run exploratory analysis to categorize cell value types (dates, strings, numbers, None) before implementing transformations.
- **Protect Non-Target Data:** Content-based filters should naturally exclude values outside the intended modification scope (e.g., skip datetime objects when transforming strings).

### Targeted Modification Strategy
- **Pattern-Driven Selection:** When cells contain mixed data types, use regex or conditional logic to identify target cells by content pattern rather than row/column position alone.
- **Null Safety:** Always check for None/null values before string operations or type conversions in iterative loops.

### Tool Selection Guidance
- **Selective Edits via OpenPyXL:** For cell-by-cell transformations within a defined range, iterate on Worksheet objects directly; pandas-to-excel workflows may lose structural elements.
